title: symphyotrichum crenifolium
intro: Symphyotrichum crenifolium, also known as mountain ironweed, is a perennial plant native to the eastern United States and Canada. It is a member of the sunflower family, known for its purple flowers and tall stature. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of mountain ironweed can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved wound healing.
